what_it_does: Describes the canvas/app package: a React + tldraw rich canvas that serves as the visual operator surface, rendering composition nodes as movable cards with semantic zoom, inspector/workshop, run controls, edge overlay, and a governed GROW loop, communicating with the bridge over a proxied /api endpoint.
observations: Provides an infinite canvas where composition nodes are movable cards
Includes an inspector, workshop, run controls, and edge overlay
Communicates with the bridge (Suite UI face) over /api, proxied
Uses one generic NodeShapeUtil to render every node type from bridge state
Claims no per-type frontend code, citing S5
Lists run and build commands (pnpm install, pnpm dev, pnpm build)
Development server runs on http://127.0.0.1:5173 and expects the bridge on :8770
Mentions a vanilla canvas/index.html as a dependency-free fallback face
Tauri desktop packaging is planned as a future wrap
Web-first approach: 'web app first, verifiable now'
standards: Generic NodeShapeUtil renders all node types from bridge state, avoiding per-type frontend code
Semantic-zoom LOD on an infinite tldraw canvas
Bridge-mediated architecture: canvas talks to the Suite UI face over /api
Tauri desktop packaging is the next planned wrap
conventions: README-style package overview with run/build instructions
Architecture description references design principles by shorthand (S5)
Uses port numbers and proxy assumptions in documentation
Distinguishes a primary rich face from a fallback vanilla face
concerns: No per-type frontend code may tightly couple rendering to the bridge state schema
Desktop packaging is future work, not current
Relies on the bridge running on a fixed port (:8770) for local development
S5 and GROW loop are referenced without definition in this file
notes: This README defines the rich canvas application as a React/tldraw visual operator surface that delegates node rendering to a generic shape util and communicates with the bridge over a proxied API.
questions: What is the governed GROW loop and how is it implemented?
What is the exact schema of the bridge state that NodeShapeUtil consumes?
What does the vanilla canvas/index.html fallback provide and how is it selected?
How is /api proxied and what endpoints are used?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches the content: it describes the canvas app, its features, run/build instructions, and its relationship to the bridge and fallback face.
apparent_themes: Generic rendering over per-type frontend code
Progressive enhancement with a dependency-free fallback
intention_signals: To create a flexible, node-based visual operator interface where the frontend stays generic and the bridge owns node semantics, while keeping a simple fallback and a path to desktop packaging.
novelty: A single generic NodeShapeUtil renders every node type from bridge state, aiming to eliminate per-node-type frontend code on a semantic-zoom tldraw canvas.
contribution: Documents the canvas/app package's role, runtime setup, build flow, and the architectural choice to render all node types through a generic shape util backed by bridge state; also records the fallback vanilla face and planned Tauri wrap.
summary_for_embedding: README for a React + tldraw infinite canvas application that renders composition nodes via a generic NodeShapeUtil, communicates with a bridge over a proxied /api, and includes run/build instructions plus a fallback HTML console.
intention_for_embedding: To explain the canvas app's visual-operator surface, its bridge-backed generic rendering strategy, and how to run, build, and eventually wrap it.
